objective: Split fitting acceptance into two named policies: physical-fit (existing strict linear-RGB thresholds, Lane B controlled captures, unchanged) and reference-synthesis-fit (Lane A public/contributor media). The Lane A objective prioritizes regional foil activation, hue ordering, highlight position, foil occupancy, relative intensity changes, texture frequency, temporal coherence (for sequences), and robust perceptual similarity — NOT absolute linear-RGB error. Do not simply lower existing thresholds; add calibration fixtures and document why each metric belongs.
current_state: reference_fitting.py has one acceptance policy: masked linear-RGB MAE inner objective with accept_error_threshold 0.06, min_accepted_sources 2, min_consistency_score 0.35. Real-media run jp-aa-fit-002 was rejected (0 sources <= 0.06, consistency 0.021) although the fit direction was plausible — absolute-RGB acceptance is miscalibrated for uncontrolled media by design.
assumptions: The frozen report schema stays intact; policy metadata may go in existing free fields or per_source regional_error maps.
Lane B physical fitting (fitting.py) is untouched.
acceptance_criteria: A named policy enum/object selects physical-fit vs reference-synthesis-fit; the CLI exposes --policy with reference-synthesis-fit default for bundle manifests.
reference-synthesis-fit acceptance uses the directive's perceptual/regional metrics with documented rationale per metric (docs/operations/reference-fit-policy.md), not rescaled RGB thresholds.
Temporal-coherence metric activates only for sequence observations; still-only sets are not penalized for lacking it.

Calibration fixtures: synthetic GOOD case (coherent multi-angle observations of a known profile, moderate exposure/compression noise) must ACCEPT under reference-synthesis-fit and may reject under physical-fit; synthetic BAD cases (wrong hue order, displaced highlight, foil on wrong regions, incoherent sources) must each REJECT under reference-synthesis-fit with the failing metric named.
Single-reference-overfit and model-limit rejections remain in force for both policies.
Existing tests pass unedited; the physical-fit path produces byte-identical decisions to current behavior.
required_tests: uv run ruff check src tests
stop_conditions: A metric cannot be computed from available observation data (document and drop with rationale, don't fake it).
expected_outputs: Calibration fixture tests (good accepts, each bad rejects with named metric)
